description: **Main processes simulated** OpenIFS 48r1 simulates the global three-dimensional atmospheric circulation together with its interactions with the land surface, sea ice and ocean wave fields. The model integrates the hydrostatic primitive equations on a terrain-following mass-based vertical coordinate, prognosing wind, temperature, specific humidity, cloud condensate (liquid, ice), precipitation species (rain, snow), cloud fraction and surface pressure. Coupled subsystems include the HTESSEL land-surface scheme (soil moisture and temperature, vegetation, snow, lakes via FLake) and the WAM ocean wave model. New at this cycle, OpenIFS 48r1 includes interactive prognostic ozone and consolidates OpenIFS and OpenIFS/AC into a single code base, with the operational CAMS chemistry and aerosol package available by default.  **Key physics and parameterizations** Cycle 48r1 represents a major upgrade of the moist physics package, restructuring the call sequence of the turbulence, shallow and deep convection, prognostic cloud and microphysics schemes for greater physical consistency. Notable changes include a new deep-convection closure with additional dependence on total advective moisture convergence; a change from exponential–exponential to random–exponential cloud vertical overlap; a vapour-deposition process for falling ice; and revised microphysics permitting supercooled drizzle drops to form and only freeze on contact with ice/snow or at much colder temperatures, enabling the prediction of freezing-drizzle events. Saturation adjustment and ice fall speed have been revised, and saturation-tendency perturbations have been removed from SPPT to suppress rare instabilities, while supersaturation adjustment has been added to ensemble SPPT perturbations with mass-weighting and a relaxation timescale. Radiation uses the ecRad scheme. The HTESSEL land surface is upgraded from a single-layer to a multi-layer snow scheme, markedly improving snowpack realism and the diurnal cycle of 2 m temperature in snow-covered regions. The dynamical core gains a new vertical finite-element discretisation, global mass fixers active for all moist species including water vapour, a revised semi-Lagrangian iterative trajectory solver, and cubic semi-Lagrangian departure-point interpolation with a 3-D quasi-monotone limiter for cloud and precipitation variables.  **Notable features of this version** Relative to the previous release (OpenIFS 43r3v2), 48r1 brings: (i) the major moist physics restructuring; (ii) the multi-layer snow scheme; (iii) interactive prognostic ozone; (iv) prediction of freezing drizzle; (v) improved water and energy conservation in the dynamics; (vi) consolidation of OpenIFS and OpenIFS/AC into a single code base with operational CAMS chemistry and aerosol available by default; (vii) a revised SPPT formulation; and (viii) updated climate fields (orography, land–sea mask, lake depth, glacier mask). Technical upgrades include migration from FCM to ecbuild/cmake, a major code restructure, single- and/or double-precision builds as default, inclusion of the Single Column Model (SCM) built alongside the 3-D model, and the ifs-test framework for known-good-output regression testing.
